puzzle.title: The Appointed Successor
puzzle.intro: A figure from ages past stands ready to step into history. When the path seemed unclear and doubt spread among the people, this servant was chosen to take up the mantle of guidance. With the weight of a sacred mission on their shoulders, they stepped forward to lead where others hesitated to follow.
clues: I was chosen to accompany a prophet and learned from their wisdom
I stood before the Ark when the promise of our Sustainer drew near, and I saw the people beginning to doubt
I was made to stand at the edge of what was promised while our leader grieved and supplicated to Allah for us
I was appointed to continue a mission that had been given to another before me
I was granted divine authority to lead the people to their destination
I succeeded in completing what was begun before me, and I received the people's affirmation of my guidance

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Yusha ibn Nun
Caleb son of Yefunneh
Eldad and Medad
Prophet Ezekiel (Hizqiyyal)
Prophet Harun (Aaron)

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: Refusing to enter the promised Holy Land
Pharaoh's armies pursuing us through the sea
The worship of the golden calf
Doubt upon hearing the Torah's command
The rebellion of Dathan and Abiram
trial.answer: Refusing to enter the promised Holy Land

location.label: Key Element
options: The Torah scroll given to Musa
The Ark of the Covenant in battle
The Jordan River crossing
The desert of forty years
Mount Sinai's revelation
location.answer: The Torah scroll given to Musa

outcome.label: Outcome
options: Entering the promised Holy Land
Building a protective wall against invaders
Constructing a sacred house of worship
Drowning in pursuit of those who denied the truth
Breaking the idols of a corrupt people

puzzle.arabic: يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱدْخُلُوا۟ ٱلْأَرْضَ ٱلْمُقَدَّسَةَ ٱلَّتِى كَتَبَ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمْ وَلَا تَرْتَدُّوا۟ عَلَىٰٓ أَدْبَارِكُمْ فَتَنقَلِبُوا۟ خَـٰسِرِينَ
puzzle.verse: O my people, enter the blessed land [i.e., Palestine] which Allāh has assigned to you and do not turn back [from fighting in Allāh's cause] and [thus] become losers." (5:21)
